boundaries.title_guess: Forest of Dean cycling & Beauchamp (John's cello course)

evidence.excerpt: Saturday 1997 April 5th. We left at 8.30 + breakfasted at the M50 turn-off to Ross, on the M5.
driven to the Park at Pedalaway. The boys cycled before lunch.

boundaries.mixed_detail: Top of A_0195_L is the END of the previous (Belgium/Gent 1996) trip: 'We sped quickly through Belgium + France... lunch at a service station, and drove on home.' The new Forest of Dean trip starts mid-page at 'Saturday 1997 April 5th.'

evidence.excerpt: Friday 18 April. We left at about 6.15 pm for Robert Grimley's installation at Bristol.
After lunch we set off home.

boundaries.title_guess: Wolverhampton camping shop & Coven canal walk (day trip)

evidence.excerpt: May 10th Saturday 11.30. Simon, Eve, John, Charles went to the camping shop beyond Wolverhampton... lunched in the van & drove to Coven... before setting off home.

boundaries.mixed_detail: A_0199_L holds two separate short outings: the May 10th Wolverhampton/Coven day trip (this row) and the May 17th Derbyshire/Calke Abbey day trip (next row), each leaving from and returning home.

evidence.excerpt: May 17th Saturday. 9.15. We set off for Derbyshire... called in at [?Caraluce], nr Alfreton & ordered van cushions.
We lunched in parklands at Calke Abbey... had tea in the van & left at 5.40.

boundaries.mixed_detail: Second outing recorded on A_0199_L; its end ('left at 5.40') falls at the top of A_0199_R, where the Wales trip then begins.

boundaries.title_guess: Wales: Arias above Harlech, Rhinog, Porthmadoc

evidence.excerpt: Saturday 24th May. ... We left at 10.35... 3.50. we arrived at [?Arias], above Harlech [?Llanbedr].
Wednesday May 28th We left at 11... We arrived home via Smethwick at 4.0.

boundaries.mixed_detail: Top of A_0199_R ends the Derbyshire/Calke Abbey day trip; the Wales trip starts mid-page at 'Saturday 24th May.'

evidence.excerpt: Saturday June 14th. We scrabbled off at 11.40. for Cleat parish hall.
In the morning we abandoned camp because of the rain & went back to B'ham for church.

boundaries.mixed_detail: A_0201_L top is the END of the Wales trip ('arrived home via Smethwick at 4.0') plus a glued-in menu slip; A_0201_R opens cleanly with the new Clent weekend.

boundaries.title_guess: Alfreton (van cushions) & Upton Jazz Festival (day outing)

evidence.excerpt: Saturday July 28th. ...Simon, Eve, John & Charles returned to Alfreton to pick up the new cushions... We went on to hear Peter play his sax in the Upton Jazz Festival.

boundaries.mixed_detail: A_0202_L contains the July 28-29 Alfreton/Upton day outing(s) (this row) and then the departure for the big France 1997 trip at 'Tuesday July 29th At 6.25. We all left for Amiens' (next row).

boundaries.title_guess: France 1997: Amiens, Loire, Bourges, Conques, Gorges du Tarn

evidence.excerpt: Tuesday July 29th At 6.25. We all left for Amiens, including Loic de Fursac, 17, the French exchange.
Hand-drawn map of France titled 'FRANCE 1997' with pink route line through Amiens, Bourges, Conques, Rhone.
After coming through the Tunnel... We picked up Tonic + arrived home at 4.30.

boundaries.mixed_detail: Starts mid-page on A_0202_L after the Alfreton/Upton day outing.

evidence.excerpt: August 22nd Friday. 5.25. William, John, Charles, Simon + Eve left home... We didn't arrive at Beauchamp until 7.0.
We went home after a cup of tea.

boundaries.mixed_detail: A_0219_R top ('We picked up Tonic + arrived home at 4.30') ends the France 1997 trip; the Aug 22 Beauchamp trip then starts, and the Sept 6 Malmesbury trip also starts further down this same page (next row).

evidence.excerpt: Saturday September 6th. 11.10. We motored to Malmesbury listening to Princess Diana's funeral on the radio.
a big lunch... Burton Hill. **** Football, wasps... long walk in & around Malmesbury.

boundaries.mixed_detail: Third dated section on A_0219_R; the preceding Beauchamp trip (Aug 22-23) ends with 'We went home after a cup of tea' before this Malmesbury departure.

boundaries.title_guess: The Anchor, East of Welland & the Malverns

evidence.excerpt: Fri 20 Sept. Simon, Eve John & Charles left home with Tonic to camp at a pub The Anchor at East[?Ington] Welland.
we drove William & Thomas back to school & set off home, arriving at 6 pm.

boundaries.mixed_detail: A_0221_L top ends the Malmesbury trip ('We left, after a very good weekend, at 3.30'); the Welland trip begins at 'Fri 20 Sept.'

evidence.excerpt: Sat October 25th. All 7 of us set off for London at 10.45... we arrived at the Lee Valley Leisure Centre Picketts Lock.
Sunday October 26th... A marvellous trip down the river... Greenwich. We visited St. Alphege's Church.

open_at_end: London/Greenwich trip starting A_0221_R is still in progress at the window edge (A_0222_R ends mid-narrative on Sunday October 26th at Greenwich, no return home recorded yet)
notes: Window opens A_0193-A_0195(top) inside the END of the Belgium/Gent 1996 trip carried over from the previous window (continues_from_previous_window). Page numbers on R pages track the book3 index (1997 entries p25-51). Several physical pages carry two trips (A_0195_L, A_0199_L, A_0199_R, A_0202_L, A_0219_R, A_0221_L) and are flagged mixed; A_0199_L and A_0219_R each open TWO trips, so two boundary rows share those page_ids. Short weekend/day outings (Wolverhampton/Coven, Derbyshire, Alfreton/Upton) are low-to-moderate confidence as standalone 'trips' since some are single-day with no overnight, but each leaves from and returns to home.
